message:
feat: Add batch compilation mode for 3-5x faster test builds

Add --batch flag to `metal0 test` that compiles all tests in a single
zig build invocation, sharing runtime module analysis across all tests.

Key changes:
- src/main/compile/batch_build.zig: Batch build file with all module defs
- incremental.zig: generateTestManifest() and batchCompileWithZigBuild()
- test_commands.zig: --batch flag and batch compilation path

How it works:
1. Copy batch_build.zig to .metal0/build.zig at runtime
2. Generate test_manifest.txt listing all .zig files to compile
3. Run `zig build` from .metal0/ directory
4. All tests share runtime module analysis (236K LOC parsed once)

Expected performance:
  Before: 390 tests × 2-3s each = 13+ minutes
  After:  1 × ~60s (shared analysis) = ~60s total
  Cached: ~0.16s per test (instant)

Usage:
  metal0 test tests/cpython --batch  # Use batch compilation
  metal0 test tests/cpython          # Normal parallel compilation
